summary:
A low volume of distribution means the drug stays mainly in blood and not much elsewhere; therefore, less medication is needed to achieve therapeutic levels. \boxed{B}

The summary accurately captures two critical aspects from Agent1’s original explanation—namely how drugs behave within different body spaces based upon their Vd value and what dosage implications this has—and maintains brevity suitable for concise communication while adhering strictly only to information provided by Agent1 without introducing any new content or assumptions beyond those explicitly stated.
